title: Tea tree spot treatment
subtitle: A 5% tea tree oil dilution in jojoba for applying to blemishes
excerpt: Tea tree essential oil diluted to 5% in jojoba oil for applying to individual blemishes. Jojoba carries the tea tree oil onto the skin without the irritation that undiluted tea tree causes. This is not a full-face treatment. Use a clean fingertip or cotton bud to apply directly to the blemish, not the surrounding skin.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): tea tree maximum facial use rate 1% for full-face, up to 5-10% for spot application on intact non-sensitive skin. Carson et al., Melaleuca alternifolia (Tea Tree) oil: a review (Clinical Microbiology Reviews, 2006).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make one bottle at a time and use within 12 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months from making. Tea tree essential oil oxidises over time and oxidised tea tree is more irritating than fresh. Store in the amber dropper bottle away from heat and light. Discard if the scent becomes sharper or more solvent-like, or if any irritation develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: The proportion of essential oil to carrier oil, expressed as a percentage of the total blend weight. A 5% dilution means 5 g of essential oil per 95 g of carrier oil. Spot treatments use a higher dilution than full-face products because they are applied to a very small area of skin for a short time. Do not apply tea tree at 5% to large skin areas.

Repeated exposure to an undiluted or high-concentration essential oil can cause the immune system to recognise the oil as an irritant, leading to a reaction on subsequent exposure even at lower concentrations. Using tea tree undiluted increases the risk of sensitisation; the 5% dilution in this recipe stays within the range considered low-risk for spot use.

content.text: Tea tree essential oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) has well-documented antimicrobial activity against the bacteria associated with acne. This recipe dilutes it to a 5%
in jojoba oil, which is appropriate for spot use on individual blemishes. Apply only to the blemish itself with a clean fingertip or cotton bud; do not spread across the whole face.
Never use tea tree essential oil undiluted on skin. Undiluted application causes irritation and, over time,
that makes future reactions more likely. Tea tree is toxic to cats; keep the bottle and application area away from pets. Do not use on children under two years of age.

items.prepNote: Golden or clear jojoba oil. Very stable carrier, non-comedogenic for most skin types.
5% of total blend weight. Melaleuca alternifolia. Fresh, not oxidised. The scent should be clean and medicinal, not solvent-like.

content.text: Weigh the jojoba oil and tea tree essential oil directly into the amber dropper bottle. Replace the dropper insert, seal the cap, and shake gently for 30 seconds to combine. Label with the making date and contents.

Dispense one drop onto a clean fingertip or cotton bud. Apply directly to the blemish and the immediately surrounding skin. Do not apply to a large area. Use morning and evening on active blemishes. Discontinue immediately if stinging, redness, or itching develops beyond the blemish area.

Store in the amber dropper bottle in a cool, dark place. Keep away from heat; a bathroom cabinet away from the shower steam is suitable. Shelf life is 12 months. Oxidised tea tree oil has a sharper, more solvent-like scent and causes more irritation. If the scent changes significantly, discard and make a fresh batch.
